← All worlds/World 03
World 03 · 1 state

Identity Crisis

Films for the strange in-between, where the version of you that worked stopped working and the new one has not arrived yet.

Choose an emotion
Not Knowing Yourself
On Not Knowing Yourself

At some point, the version of you that was working stopped working. The story you told about who you were — the reliable one, the driven one, the one who had figured it out — became a costume you kept putting on even after it stopped fitting. Identity crisis sounds clinical. It does not feel clinical. It feels like standing in front of a mirror and not recognising the person looking back. These films live inside that strangeness — the doubled self, the unrecognisable reflection — and stay there long enough for it to become something you can hold.

Not Knowing Yourself

Films for the path that disappeared mid-walk.

🏆 Won 1 Academy Award

CS9.0
1999

Fight Club

David Fincher

View Film →

A sleepless office worker meets a charismatic stranger and starts a bare-knuckle club in the basement of a bar. Identity, capitalism, and self-destruction tangle from there.

CS8.8
2010

Black Swan

Darren Aronofsky

View Film →

A perfectionist ballerina at a New York company wins the lead in Swan Lake — and finds the role asking her to become someone she can’t quite stay.

CS9.1
1998

The Truman Show

Peter Weir

View Film →

A man who has unknowingly lived his entire life inside a televised, scripted reality slowly begins to suspect the truth and look for the door.

CS8.5
2001

Donnie Darko

Richard Kelly

View Film →

A troubled teenager in 1988 sleepwalks, sees visions of a sinister rabbit, and tries to make sense of a future he may or may not be able to change.

CS9.5
1966

Persona

Ingmar Bergman

View Film →

A famous actress goes silent mid-performance and is sent to recover in a remote cottage with a young nurse. The two women slowly begin to blur.

CS9.1
2022

Everything Everywhere All at Once

Daniels

View Film →

A laundromat owner in the middle of a tax audit discovers she’s the only person who can save the multiverse — by accessing every life she didn’t live.

CS8.7
2008

Synecdoche, New York

Charlie Kaufman

View Film →

A theatre director awarded a vast grant tries to build a life-size replica of his life inside a New York warehouse. The project, like its maker, never finishes.

CS8.4
2009

Mr. Nobody

Jaco Van Dormael

View Film →

The last mortal man on Earth, aged 118, recounts every possible life he could have lived from the moment his parents separated when he was nine.

CS8.4
2013

Enemy

Denis Villeneuve

View Film →

A history professor discovers an actor who is his exact double, and the obsession to meet him unravels both of their lives in slow, surreal increments.

CS9.0
2020

Soul

Pete Docter

View Film →

A middle-school music teacher who finally lands the gig of his dreams dies on the same day — and finds himself in a celestial waystation, trying to get back.

CS9.3
2001

Mulholland Drive

David Lynch

View Film →

An aspiring actress arrives in Hollywood. A woman with amnesia following a car crash. A director being coerced. Reality and dream collapse after the Silencio scene.

CS8.8
2002

Adaptation

Spike Jonze

View Film →

A screenwriter struggles to adapt a book about orchids and becomes the subject of his own unfinished script. His twin brother writes shamelessly entertaining movies.

CS8.7
1999

Being John Malkovich

Spike Jonze

View Film →

A puppeteer discovers a portal into John Malkovich’s mind. He and others use it for different purposes. Identity is not the point. Or is.

CS8.5
1999

The Talented Mr. Ripley

Anthony Minghella

View Film →

A poor young man in 1950s New York is sent to Italy to retrieve a rich man’s son. He decides it would be easier to become him instead.

CS8.3
2020

I'm Thinking of Ending Things

Charlie Kaufman

View Film →

A woman accompanies her boyfriend to his parents’ farm. Her inner monologue and the external reality begin to diverge. What is happening becomes the question.

CS9.2
2018

Burning

Lee Chang-dong

View Film →

A young man in Seoul reconnects with a childhood acquaintance who returns from Africa with a mysterious new boyfriend who confesses to an unusual pastime.

CS8.7
2013

Under the Skin

Jonathan Glazer

View Film →

An alien in the form of a woman drives through Glasgow picking up men. The film is what she observes and what begins to change in her.

CS8.7
2018

Annihilation

Alex Garland

View Film →

Five women enter a mysterious zone called the Shimmer. The film follows what the Shimmer does to identity, memory, and biology.

CS8.4
2013

The Double

Richard Ayoade

View Film →

A meek office worker in a dystopian bureaucracy finds his exact double has joined the company and is everything he is not — and begins to replace him.

CS8.4
2000

American Psycho

Mary Harron

View Film →

A Wall Street banker in 1980s New York performs identity through business cards, restaurants, and violence, real or imagined.

CS8.2
2001

A Beautiful Mind

Ron Howard

View Film →

Mathematical genius John Nash develops schizophrenia. The film follows his mind before and after the diagnosis.

CS8.2
2004

The Machinist

Brad Anderson

View Film →

A machinist who has not slept in a year begins to believe a mysterious stranger is trying to frame him. His body is almost gone.

CS8.8
2016

Silence

Martin Scorsese

View Film →

Two Jesuit priests travel to 17th-century Japan in search of their missing mentor. What they find tests the identity they built on faith.

CS9.3
2011

A Separation

Asghar Farhadi

View Film →

An Iranian couple separates over whether to leave the country. The dispute spirals into a legal and moral crisis that implicates everyone.

Social Anxiety →

For when the room becomes a stage you never agreed to.

Films don't just entertain. They quietly confirm you were seen — long before anyone in your real life knew how to.

18 emotional states·277 curated films·Growing weekly
